title: Sugarcane Processing — Node Cutting and Bud Viability
content: Sugarcane bud cutter sections mature stalks at inter-node positions, preserving 1–2 buds per sett for planting. Blade must cut perpendicular to stalk axis within ±2 mm of node to maximise germination rate (> 85%).
extended_content: Stalk diameter: 25–45 mm; cutting force F = σ_shear × π × d²/4 ≈ 25 MPa × 850 mm² = 21 kN (requires hydraulic or heavy-duty motor). Practical: circular blade (300 mm diameter, 3 mm kerf, tungsten carbide tipped) driven at 3000 RPM. Motorised guide: conveyor feeds stalks at 0.1–0.2 m/s. Sensor detects node (optical reflectance difference husk vs node 20% Δ reflectance) to trigger blade position. Sett length: 250–300 mm (2 buds). Germination rate vs cut position: centre of inter-node → 92%, near node → 78% (heat/bruising risk). Disinfection: blade dip in 5% sodium hypochlorite between rows prevents ratoon stunting disease spread.
formula: F_cut = σ_shear · π·d²/4 ;  sett_length = n_buds · inter-node_length
units: F: N, d: mm, σ: MPa
source: Obaido et al., Sugarcane Sett Cutting, Biosystems Engineering 2019; ISSCT Mechanisation Proceedings; FAO Sugarcane Production Manual
